name: two_sum
problem: Given a list of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return the indices (i, j) with i < j such that nums[i] + nums[j] == target. You may assume exactly one solution exists.
signature: def two_sum(nums: list[int], target: int) -> tuple[int, int]:

name: is_palindrome
problem: Return True iff `s` is a palindrome when considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring case.
public_tests: assert is_palindrome('A man, a plan, a canal: Panama') is True
assert is_palindrome('race a car') is False

name: merge_intervals
problem: Given a list of intervals [start, end], merge all overlapping intervals and return the result sorted by start.

name: column_maximim_strategy
problem: Given a numpy matrix A, find a probability vector as another column numpy matrix p, such that there exist no probability vector q satisfies min(A@q) > min(A@p)
